title: [v5.4-rhel] Bump runc to v1.2.8 - CVE-2025-52881
body:
<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

For more detailed information, please review our contributing guidelines:
https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests
-->
Bump runc to v1.3.3 to address CVE-2025-52881
Also fixes CVE-2025-52565 and CVE-2025-31133

Fixes: https://issues.redhat.com/browse/RHEL-126912, https://issues.redhat.com/browse/RHEL-126632

With selinux getting bumped, a number of functions from the
old label directory in selinux have moved upwards.  This commit
adjust for those changes.
#### Checklist

Ensure you have completed the following checklist for your pull request to be reviewed:
<!-- Use [x] to mark as done, or click the checkbox after opening PR -->

- [x ] Certify you wrote the patch or otherwise have the right to pass it on as an open-source patch by signing all
commits. (`git commit -s`). (If needed, use `git commit -s --amend`).  The author email must match
the sign-off email address. See [CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#sign-your-prs)
for more information.
- [x ] Referenced issues using `Fixes: #00000` in commit message (if applicable)
- [ x] [Tests](https://github.com/containers/podman/tree/main/test#readme) have been added/updated (or no tests are needed)
- [ x] [Documentation](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/docs/README.md) has been updated (or no documentation changes are needed)
- [ x] All commits pass `make validatepr` (format/lint checks)
- [ x] [Release note](https://github.com/kubernetes/community/blob/master/contributors/guide/release-notes.md) entered in the section below (or `None` if no user-facing changes)

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
Write `None` if there are no user-facing changes, otherwise enter your release note below.
Include "action required" if users need to take action when upgrading.
-->

```release-note
None
```

comments.body:
Someone has to cherry-pick https://github.com/containers/podman/commit/2cfd526ec8e3e37d6e3f26ef7cbe93f7d8a5ddd6 and https://github.com/containers/podman/commit/f06f77468de132505c76d834ec4b275e70fc1873 like I did in https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/27464 and https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/27465 for 5.6-rhel

This would need to happen for all still active rhel branches I think. Or well at least the ones with an aarch64 build
@Luap99 looks like green tests, I will add the selinux commit if you think that's still needed.

@TomSweeneyRedHat well there are no tests here other than a build test.

The selinux bump is needed because the runc bump only addresses the apparmor write which we (RHEL) don't care about as it doesn't use apparmor. The selinux one is actually the critical part as that is what RHEL uses by default.
For selinux we will want https://github.com/opencontainers/selinux/pull/242 first, without it it means having to update and fix storage first for the new securejoin API then also vendor it here which of course is a lot more work.
So it would be great if @kolyshkin and @rhatdan could make a new release with the downgraded selinux to make the backports much easier.

I guess the other alternative is to go mod replace github.com/cyphar/filepath-securejoin here to the lower 0.5.1 version.
https://github.com/opencontainers/selinux/releases/tag/v1.13.1 is released so you can vendor that
nit: technically, `[v5.4-rhel] Bump opencontainers/selinux to v1.13.1` and `[v5.4-rhel] Make adjustments for selinux changes` should be in a single commit (or we break git bisect).

And the title should say `[v5.4-rhel] Bump runc to v1.2.8` not v1.3.3.

Other than that, LGTM(nb).

@kir I'll try combining the two commits as suggested.  I'd tried juggling them late last week, and bits got strewn across the test floor.  Will see.  Thanks for the title typo too.  I caught and fixed that in the commit title, but not the PRs.
Repushed after combining the selinux bump to 1.13.1 and the changes required from it.
